project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling on a vacant lot in the Neighborhood Residential (NR) District, consistent with the district's purpose of accommodating single-family detached residential dwellings at low densities.

Add an accessory dwelling unit above a detached garage on the same lot in the Neighborhood Residential (NR) District, providing an additional housing option as contemplated by the district's diverse-housing purpose.

Convert the single-family lot into an eight-unit multi-family apartment building in the Neighborhood Residential (NR) District, a use the district's regulations discourage as substantially interfering with the quiet single-family residential nature of the district.

Open a professional office with ground-floor retail space in the General Mixed-Use (GMU) District, consistent with the district's purpose of providing professional offices and light-to-moderate intensity commercial uses proximate to residential districts.

Build a large-format warehouse club with an outdoor truck marshaling yard in the General Mixed-Use (GMU) District, a scale and intensity of use out of character with the district's purpose of a compatible mixed-use environment integrating residential, commercial, and institutional uses.

Open a small-scale light manufacturing and distribution facility with minimal exterior movement of vehicles and materials in the Light Industrial (IL) District, consistent with the district's purpose of accommodating small-scale light manufacturing, assembly, fabrication, and distribution uses.

Add a research and development annex with expanded loading docks to the existing light industrial building in the Light Industrial (IL) District, increasing the site's exterior vehicle and materials movement.

Build a large-scale heavy manufacturing plant with extensive exterior movement of vehicles, materials, and goods and greater potential for adverse environmental impacts in the Light Industrial (IL) District, a use the district's own purpose text reserves for the more intensive Industrial (IN) District instead.

Renovate an existing building's ground floor into retail space with upper-floor residential units in the T4 Sub-District (General Urban) of the Downtown D1 District, consistent with its purpose of a predominantly residential urban fabric with a mix of uses at moderately intense urbanism.

Build a new multi-family building at 140 dwelling units per acre in the T4 Sub-District (General Urban) of the Downtown D1 District, exceeding the sub-district's maximum residential density of 24 to 55 dwelling units per acre under Table 40.2-207.
Open a retail cannabis dispensary selling recreational marijuana products to the public.
Build a hyperscale data center campus with on-site backup power generation and cooling towers.
